title: Coherent Particle Sampling: Verifier-Free Inference-Time Scaling for Diffusion Models via Prediction Self-Consistency
description: We propose Coherent Particle Sampling (CoPS), a training-free method that enables inference-time scaling for diffusion and flow matching models without requiring any external reward model or verifier. CoPS generates multiple sampling trajectories (particles) in parallel and uses the temporal coherence of intermediate denoised predictions as an intrinsic quality signal to guide particle resampling, grounded in the theoretical connection between prediction stability and ODE discretization error.
motivation: Current inference-time scaling methods for diffusion models (FK steering, SMC-based approaches) require external reward models (CLIP, aesthetic predictors, task-specific verifiers) to select among candidate sampling trajectories. This creates dependencies on reward model availability, introduces reward hacking risks, adds computational overhead, and limits applicability to domains without suitable verifiers. We observe that the diffusion model's own denoised predictions carry an underexploited quality signal: well-converging trajectories produce temporally coherent predictions, while poor trajectories exhibit erratic prediction changes. This intrinsic signal can replace external rewards for particle selection.
proposed_approach: CoPS works in three stages: (1) Generate K particle trajectories in parallel using any standard sampler (DDIM, Euler, DPM-Solver). (2) At designated resampling steps, compute the Prediction Coherence Score (PCS) for each particle — measuring how smoothly the model's denoised estimate x_hat_0 evolves across timesteps using a distance metric (L2, LPIPS, or cosine in feature space). (3) Resample particles proportional to exp(alpha * PCS), favoring trajectories with smooth prediction convergence. Additionally, we propose Adaptive Step Allocation (ASA), which uses PCS to redistribute timestep budgets within a fixed NFE budget — allocating more steps to high-curvature regions. The theoretical justification is that PCS directly measures the local truncation error of the ODE solver: lower PCS variance implies lower discretization error and higher sample quality.

related_work.title: A General Framework for Inference-time Scaling and Steering of Diffusion Models
related_work.authors: Singhal, Horvitz, Teehan, Ren, Yu, McKeown, Ranganath
related_work.relation: Establishes FK steering with external rewards as potentials. CoPS replaces external rewards with intrinsic PCS, eliminating the verifier dependency.

related_work.title: Feynman-Kac Correctors in Diffusion: Annealing, Guidance, and Product of Experts
related_work.authors: Skreta, Akhound-Sadegh, Ohanesian, Bondesan, Aspuru-Guzik, Doucet, Brekelmans, Tong, Neklyudov
related_work.relation: Provides principled PDE-based corrections for particle sampling but still relies on external reward/guidance signals.

related_work.title: Scaling Inference Time Compute for Diffusion Models
related_work.authors: Ma, Tong, Jia, Hu, Su, Zhang, Yang, Li, Jaakkola, Jia, Xie
related_work.relation: Demonstrates inference-time scaling with search + verifiers. We show scaling is possible without verifiers.

related_work.title: Self-Rectifying Diffusion Sampling with Perturbed-Attention Guidance
related_work.authors: Ahn, Cho, Min, Jang, Kim, Kim, Park, Jin, Kim
related_work.relation: Training-free guidance using attention perturbation. Operates on single trajectories; CoPS operates on trajectory selection.

related_work.title: Improving Sample Quality of Diffusion Models Using Self-Attention Guidance
related_work.relation: Self-attention based guidance. Single-trajectory method; orthogonal and combinable with CoPS.

related_work.title: CFG-Zero*: Improved Classifier-Free Guidance for Flow Matching Models
related_work.authors: Fan, Chen, Chen, Kang, Zhu, Wen
related_work.relation: Improves CFG for flow matching. CoPS complements CFG by adding trajectory-level selection.

related_work.title: Variance-Reduction Guidance: Sampling Trajectory Optimization for Diffusion Models
related_work.relation: Optimizes trajectory offline by reducing prediction error variance. CoPS performs online particle resampling using prediction coherence.

related_work.title: Classifier-Free Diffusion Guidance
related_work.relation: Standard conditional guidance method. CoPS is orthogonal — works at trajectory selection level, not per-step guidance.

related_work.title: Diffusion Models without Classifier-free Guidance
related_work.relation: Removes CFG via modified training. CoPS is training-free and addresses a different problem (inference-time scaling).

related_work.title: VFScale: Intrinsic Reasoning through Verifier-Free Test-time Scalable Diffusion Model
related_work.relation: Verifier-free scaling but for discrete reasoning tasks (Maze/Sudoku), requires training modifications. CoPS is training-free and targets image generation.

hypothesis: The temporal coherence of intermediate denoised predictions (x_hat_0) along a diffusion sampling trajectory is a reliable intrinsic quality signal: selecting trajectories with higher prediction coherence, without any external reward model, achieves generation quality comparable to reward-guided particle sampling methods.
success_criteria: CoPS achieves FID within 5% of CLIP-guided particle sampling on COCO 5K
CoPS achieves CLIP/ImageReward scores within 3% of reward-guided methods
CoPS significantly outperforms random particle selection (>10% FID improvement)
PCS shows strong rank correlation (Spearman rho > 0.5) with external quality metrics across particles within the same prompt
Quality scales positively with number of particles under constant total NFE budget
Adaptive step allocation provides measurable improvement over fixed schedules under the same NFE budget
CoPS is compatible with and complementary to existing guidance methods (CFG, PAG, SAG)
